question_text: Why does a sharp knife cut better than a blunt knife?

options.label: A
options.text: It applies less force to the material

options.label: B
options.text: It has a smaller area, so the same force creates higher pressure

options.label: C
options.text: The material is weaker near sharp edges

correct_answer: It has a smaller area, so the same force creates higher pressure
explanation: A sharp knife has a much smaller surface area in contact with the material being cut compared to a blunt knife. Since pressure is calculated using the formula P = F/A (pressure equals force divided by area), when the same force is applied with a smaller area, the pressure is much higher. This higher pressure makes it easier to cut through the material.
